entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture and how to get started
entry.content:
Great article Taryn.

Being on the road for almost the same time than you and currently leading the IT Strategy for a retail company, I would like to endorse your words.

I strongly agree with the emphasis on selecting architecture artifacts based on the needs of stakeholders rather than adhering strictly to architectural frameworks for their own sake. In my experience, aligning architecture work products—such as principles, standards, and guidelines—with the specific business requirements and objectives is critical for ensuring relevance and buy-in from both business and IT stakeholders. This approach also fosters collaboration and reduces friction when it comes to implementing changes or improvements.

Furthermore, I appreciate the acknowledgment that architecture projects are rarely linear. The iterative nature of the process is essential for adapting to evolving business needs and technology landscapes, and this flexibility helps ensure that we can adjust to new insights, whether they come from user feedback, evolving market conditions, or new technologies.

Much have changed in the last 30 years. But much stays the same, like these principles.

entry.title: Re: Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Maintenance Task Lists via API, OAuth and Postman
entry.content:

Would it possible to test the Scenario 2 in the "Try Out" feature in the "SAP Business Accelerator Hub"? We have the same requirement to that scenario only that the Business Object will be the Maintenance Order. We were able to connect our lower environment and successfully responding for GET function. For POST I believe there are need some more things to do. Does SAP have tutorial on it?

Hopefully you can help. Thanks.

entry.content:
My name is Artyom and i’m excited to join SAP Community

I already had experience working in SAP as business analyst several years ago. My specialization was finance, audit and procurement. After couple years away from the SAP platform, I would like to pursue my career in SAP as a consultant with new knowledge.

Best regards,
Artyom

entry.title: How to perform POST using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to achieve this scenario: Maintenance Order already exists in the system and API Attachment is used to upload an attachment to S/4HANA Cloud, assign it to and create a DIR, then assign the DIR to the Maintenance Order.

We want to perform the POST operation using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature since we were able to connect our Test system and was able to perform the GET operation.

Can you help us how to test that?

The POST function is under: Document Management > /AttachmentContentSet > Uploads attachment to a business object

See reference blog below that is technically it is possible but was on different method: Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Mai... - SAP Community

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: What’s New in Job Monitoring for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
What’s New in Job Monitoring for SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve as an indispensable tool for managing and monitoring SAP landscapes. The latest updates to Job Monitoring bring significant enhancements, enabling organizations to efficiently manage automated processes, improve transparency, and streamline operations.

This blog explores these updates in depth, offering insights into how they can empower businesses to optimize their IT operations and adapt to evolving business needs.

Introduction to Job Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM

In SAP environments, managing scheduled jobs and background tasks across multiple systems can be challenging. SAP Cloud ALM's Job Monitoring simplifies this by providing a centralized view of all scheduled processes, alerts for failures or delays, and actionable insights to improve operational efficiency.

The recent updates build on this foundation with advanced features such as granular alerting, enhanced notifications, new job types for monitoring, and an intuitive user interface. Let’s dive into the details.

Key Features and Enhancements

1. Comprehensive Notifications for Better Insights

Keeping stakeholders informed is critical. The enhanced notification system in SAP Cloud ALM now provides detailed insights into job execution:

  • Run Information: Notifications include the start time, end time, and a unique Run ID.
  • Metrics: Execution status and application status are displayed to identify issues quickly.
  • Execution Details: Information on the executable and who executed it is included for better traceability.

Chat notifications have also been improved, enabling users to access job metrics directly within chat tools for faster collaboration.

2. Advanced Monitoring Options

Monitor Identity Provisioning Jobs

With the latest updates, you can now monitor the job execution data of SAP Cloud Identity Services, specifically the Identity Provisioning service type. This allows better tracking and management of identity-related tasks across your organization.

Support for SAP Business Workflow Monitoring

Job Monitoring now includes SAP Business Workflow jobs, broadening the scope of monitored job types.

Job Type-Specific Monitoring

Administrators can monitor jobs based on the type, such as:

  • SAP Application Jobs
  • SAP ABAP Jobs
  • SAP Business Workflow Jobs

To focus on specific processes, data collection can be enabled or disabled for individual job types within managed components.

3. Flexible Alerting and Event Management

SAP Cloud ALM introduces a series of enhancements to alerting and event handling, enabling users to respond proactively to issues:

Customizable Alerts for Execution Status

Alerts can now be restricted to specific execution statuses, such as:

  • Red (critical): Indicating severe issues.
  • Yellow (warning): Highlighting potential problems.

Users can configure alerts based on their operational priorities, minimizing unnecessary notifications.

New Alert Types

Two new alert types have been added:

  • Critical Runtime: Raised when a job exceeds the expected runtime.
  • Critical Delay: Triggered for significant delays in job execution.

Event Filtering by Job Scheduler

A new "Scheduled By" filter allows events to be filtered based on the user who scheduled the job, adding granularity to monitoring.

4. Redesigned User Interface for Simplified Navigation

Home Page Redesign

The Overview Page has been renamed to Home and revamped to display:

  • Job metrics and insights tailored to the selected scope and time frame.
  • Alerts and automated actions grouped by service or system.

This redesign improves usability and provides a clearer overview of monitored processes.

Improved Scope Selector

The updated scope selector offers a more intuitive interface, making it easier for users to define and switch between scopes.

5. Enhanced Analytics and Reporting

New Analysis Page

A tabular Analysis Page offers deeper insights into job performance, including:

  • Average Runtime: Helps identify performance trends.
  • Failure Rates: Pinpoints problem areas.
  • Total Executions: Highlights job volumes for a given period.

Grouped Job View

Jobs are now grouped in the Monitoring Page, making it easier to analyze related jobs collectively. Grouped jobs are marked for easy identification.

6. Streamlined Configuration and Management

Global Settings Management

Administrators can:

  • View housekeeping and aggregation jobs.
  • Configure the retention time for aggregated executions based on business requirements.

Removing Configured Services or Systems

The Remove Configuration feature allows administrators to delete all configurations for a service or system, ensuring a clean setup.

7. Event Handling and Automation

The latest updates enable greater automation and flexibility in event handling:

  • Triggering Event Actions: Configure event actions such as email notifications, chat messages, or automated workflows for specific job events.
  • Action on Every Occurrence: Raise events (e.g., emails, alerts) for every red or yellow status occurrence.

Precision Timing for Event Actions

Previously limited to minutes, event actions can now be configured in seconds, minutes, or hours, providing more control over event response times.

8. Calendar View for Service Events

The Home Page now includes a calendar icon on each card, enabling users to view:

  • Ongoing events for services and systems.
  • Scheduled events within the next hour.

Practical Use Cases

Scenario 1: Ensuring Timely Identity Provisioning

An organization can monitor SAP Cloud Identity Services jobs to ensure that identity-related tasks (e.g., user creation) are executed without delay. Alerts for critical delays or failures ensure immediate corrective action.

Scenario 2: Managing Large Job Volumes

Using the new analysis tools, administrators can identify trends in job runtime and failure rates, optimizing system performance by addressing bottlenecks.

Scenario 3: Enhancing SLA Adherence

By configuring alerts for critical runtimes or execution delays, businesses can improve adherence to service-level agreements (SLAs).

Key Benefits of These Updates

  • Enhanced Efficiency: Automate repetitive monitoring tasks and focus on exceptions.
  • Improved Collaboration: Notifications and alerts provide actionable insights to all stakeholders.
  • Proactive Issue Management: Address potential failures before they escalate.
  • Scalability: Monitor a variety of job types and integrate them seamlessly across services.

Conclusion

The latest updates to Job Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM demonstrate SAP's commitment to innovation and operational excellence. With detailed analytics, flexible alerting, and streamlined user interfaces, SAP Cloud ALM empowers businesses to optimize their IT landscapes and achieve better outcomes.

Explore these features today to see how they can transform your operations. For more information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

 

Keep learning and keep sharing.

Cheers!

entry.title: Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
In the S/4HANA public cloud, is it possible to recognize revenue based on the Incoterms from sales orders? We've been advised to use the "POD relevant" checkbox in the Business Partner master data, but we have customers whose Incoterms vary with each order. This makes it impractical to manage as a manual process.

Our ideal scenario would be to recognize revenue immediately for certain Incoterms, while deferring recognition until delivery for others.

Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: How to Identify CDS Views as Alternatives to Classic...
entry.content:
Hi Priyanka,

My apologies. I thought you're looking for "extractors."

Anyhow, from the SAP Help link above, there's a link to the CDS Views available by Line of Business.

With that said, there is no one to one replacement of classic extractors to CDS Views.

For example, if you're looking for 0FI_GL_4, you can try to do a search and it'll give you options like I_JournalEntry.

Hope this helps.

Kind Regards,

Michael

entry.title: Configuring Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) In SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to predict the Probability Of Failure (PoF) for a technical object associated with a specific failure mode based on its age?. In this blog, I would like to highlight the essential features of Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) in SAP Asset Performance Management (APM).

Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) is a feature within APM that allows the maintenance team to predict when a technical object could fail and how likely  failure would occur given the age of the technical object. The graph that FCA produces is based on Weibull model, a commonly used data model within the realm of reliability. System uses malfunction notifications to train and score the FCA model and visualize the failure data. On the graph, we can view upper and lower confidence intervals (the range in values of likely failure given the technical object`s age) and Probability Of Failure (PoF).

SAP Asset Performance Management

My Business Use Case:

We run an air compressor with a standby equipment continuously to operate the kiln girth gear lubrication system. As a maintenance engineer, I would like to predict the Probability Of Failure (PoF) using Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) in SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) for one of the air compressors before the planned outage in Jul 2025.

My Technical Object Structure in S/4 HANA Asset Management:

MTTR/MTBR Analysis for the technical object 10001729 in S/4 HANA Asset Management:

Before we dive into Failure Curve Analytics, I would also like to touch base on other alternatives to predict next failure date for the technical object 10001729 using MTBF(Mean Time Between Failure) average values. 

  • I assume failure occurs at regular intervals and the next failure date is simply:

  • I assume failure occurs randomly over time and use random model with exponential distribution to predict next failure date:

 

Now, let us dive into Failure Curve Analytics and see when the Air Compressor will fail if it runs continuously.

First things first, check the integrity of the Air Compressor 10001729 in SAP APM.

To view the actual failure curve graph, we need to create a FCA model. FCA model needs notifications to train and score. For now, all the notifications & failure modes that will be used to train and score have already been created in the S/4 HANA Asset Management system that is associated with the SAP APM system. 

Create a Technical Object Group and assign the respective technical objects which are being used for FCA model.

 

 

Creating Failure Curve Analytics Model:

 

If we have any model configurations, we can see them once we click on "Configure Failure Curve Analytics".

I have created a new FCA model as,

  • The age for conditional probability of failure is used to calculate the conditional probability of the failure. For example, if you enter 100 days as the age and your technical object has a current age of 50 days, then the conditional probability of failure is calculated for the age of 100 days given that the technical object has not failed until the age of 50 days. If you leave blank, then system calculates only the probability of failure.
  • First Installation date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its first failure. If installation date is not maintained, the first failure date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its next failure.
  • If technical object is repairable and restored to a "like-new" condition and set to the age of 0 once it is repaired. This age is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its next failure. If not repairable, the installation date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its failure.

Under Input Data Sets, let us assign the technical objects that are being trained and scored within the FCA model. We can "Add" or "Remove" the technical objects from the model.

Now, let us assign the failure modes. All the failure modes associated with the failure data profile of the technical object used in FCA model are displayed. We can check off the failure modes to be excluded from training and scoring of the model.

Note: It is considered to have no more than 40 failure modes associated with the model.

Now let us specify the malfunction notification date range to train and score the FCA model. A default date range is provided by the system, although this can be altered if desired.

 

Training and Scoring the Model:

With all the input Data Sets properly configured, the FCA model AC_SL200_FCA can now be trained and scored.

If the training and scoring fails, system displays the failure list that prevented the model from completing successful training and scoring and the status will be set to "Failed".

If the training and scoring of the model are both successful, the status of the both will be set to "Completed".

 

If we want to run the model at a different interval, we can still change the interval to DAY, WEEK & MONTH.

 

Viewing the Analytics Graph of the FCA Model:

Now the FCA Model "AC_SL200_FCA" has been successfully trained and scored. The FCA analytical graph can be viewed within the technical object details page. 

Let us navigate to "Explore Technical Objects" tile.

 

on the graph, there are three curves. The main one is probability of failure curve, which is the likely percent chance the equipment 10001729 will fail because of the failure mode "Low Discharge Pressure" selected at a specified age in days. The upper and lower confidence intervals mark the highest and lowest percent chance that the equipment 10001729 will fail due to the "Low Discharge Pressure" failure mode. The predicted failure date is Jan 8, 2025, and the time to failure is  47 days.

Key Note:

The MTBF approach to predict next failure date based on average values can mask variations and specific trends in the failure data. They assume constant failure rate, and might not reflect reality.

The FCA with Weibull distribution is more flexible and can represent various failure modes and can model different failure behaviors over time. When we consider systems and their components, they often do not fail at constant rate, So the FCA analysis can account for varying failure rates over time and fits the actual failure data to a curve and predicts when future failures might occur, where as the MTBF cannot. 

This brings me to the end of my process walk-through. I hope this blog proves useful to Plant Maintenance team. I look forward to seeing Plant Maintenance team using SAP APM Failure Curve Analytics to predict the technical object failure. 

If you would like to keep up on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, Asset Management and Asset Performance Management, follow me on SAP Community and LinkedIn.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

My LinkedIn Profile: www.linkedin.com/in/pardhreddyc

entry.title: Re: Basic Start Date of Preventive Maintenance Order keep...
entry.content:
Hey lmoc,

If you want to get the most accurate answer, please provide details of the relevant maintenance plan for this work order. By details I mean scheduling parameters, cycle information and if manual calls have been done. 

First things first - this is normal, the WO gets re-scheduled numerous times. Maybe it is performance-based maintenance plan? So the system considers your measurement readings and adapts the plan accordingly. 

Please share screenshots so our community can help you better.

Cheers mate, Dan

entry.content:

I'm following the documentation but when I'm creating the Dock Container it says Timeout

after a while

I'm using Windows 11, 32gb RAM and 250gb ssd space left to use. Any suggestion please. Thanks!

Thanks and best wishes,

Willy.

EDIT
I had to assign more memory. I set it to 20gb. Thanks!

entry.title: QA08 Activate despite of Stock is not creating NEW inspection lot for QI stock without lots
entry.content:
Hi Everybody,

We are moving material from Plant A of Company C1 to Plant B of Company C2 via Purchase Order.

1. When the material is received at Plant B, there should be no Inspection Lot, and the material should go to UNRESTRICTED

2. This should happen only if the supplier is Plant A

3. In case of any other vendor, the system should create Inspection Lot and move the stock to QI stock

We have made the following settings:

1. Inspection Type 01 is active for the material

2. Post to insp stock check box is checked for Inspection Type 01

3. QM proc active check box is not checked in QM view of Material Master

4. Q-Info record is maintained for material, Plant A and Plant B combination

5. In the Q-Info record Insp. controls has the value 'No Inspection'

6. GR is with reference to Inbound Delivery in VL32N.

7. We have Packing and Handling Units linked

The requirement is as follows.

1. Before implementing the above said scenario for some reason stock went to QI without lots and we need this to get transferred into unrestricted or to create valid inspection lot.

2. I expected QA08 transaction can help us in create inspection lots by using 'Activate despite of stock' option with inspection type '01', '08' or '0800'.

3. But this didn't create NEW lot for QI stocks without lots.

Is QA08 really has this functionality?

Since we are working with Inbound delivery and packing with HU, reverse posting of document is hectic.

Any work around?

 

Thanks, With Regards,

Dinesh Kumar.

entry.title: Re: A Step by Step process to trigger Odata Service to send data based on Event Configuration in SAP
entry.content:
Great blog

Is it possible to create a wrapper program job in S4 and trigger the Odata service push to CPI?

Regards

entry.title: Re: European Sustainability Reporting Standards(ESRS) Journey With SAP Sustainability Control Tower(
entry.content:
Hi Leticia,

Thank you! I appreciate your kind words.

1) This question is about SCT data collection in general, not specifically to ESRS. I have read that for the measure amount you can import data from S/4 HANA. However, you can only do it once, and then the task would be marked as complete, right?  How would you handle an scenario with multiple ERPs, for example, for a complex multinational? Is there a way to import from multiple ERPs?

=> Yes, once the data is imported without errors, the import status becomes "published". If a customer has multiple ERP systems, and does not want to go with the file upload method, I would recommend using either SAP Datasphere or Inbound APIs. For GHG emissions, If the customer has SAP EHSM-Environment Management, the CO2e calculation for each business location can be done in EHSM-EM, though the customer maintains multiple ERPS. Once the calculation is done in EHSM-EM, customer can import the emissions data using the option "Import From SAP EHS Management".

2) About ESRS, do the metrics include the ESRS EFRAG Code? Is it possible to include it in a report?

=> If I understand correctly, you are asking how to get the ESRS E1,E2,E3 etc., codes for disclosure requirement in ESRS report that is being generated from SAP SCT? - If so, the report has already got the disclosure requirement codes.

3) You included the following comment in your blog "Please bear in mind that the transition of ESRS journey with SAP SCT is underway".  What does this mean exactly and when would the transition be completed?

=> I mean, SAP is working to fully align the SCT metrics with the disclosure requirements of the ESRS definitions. We need to check the SCT Road Map Explorer to get more insights on it. Expecting more content in Q1 2025.

4) Are you familiar with the ESRS Disclosure Management Content from PAPM (ESRS Disclosure Management | SAP Help Portal)?. Have you explored it?

=> Yes, I explored. It`s a different cloud solution and covers all 12 standards of ESRS disclosure requirements. Right now, I am focusing mainly on SAP SCT to achieve ESRS & EU taxonomy reporting.

--

Regards,

Pardha Reddy.C

entry.title: Project Stock and Closing of WBS
entry.content:
Good day SAP MM/PM Experts.

I would like to know if a WBS can be closed although there is an open qty of Project Stock sitting in the plant/sloc?

I understand that when there are open qtys in project stock "Q", the WBS is not charged yet for the material, and the value is sitting as a statistical expense under the WBS.

Does that allow the WBS to be closed even though the project has not taken their stock yet?

I hope I made my question clear.

Any advice would be appreciated.

Thanks.

Neeren

MM (Materials Management) 

SAP Project and Resource Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Data retrieval inconsistency
entry.content: SAP doesn't offer any official way to access tables generically. Solutions: access the database directly (custom solution) instead of going to it indirectly through the ABAP system or develop a custom RFC-enabled function module. For more information about RFC_READ_TABLE, see the note https://me.sap.com/notes/382318.

entry.title: Mmoexp POE currency: Traps and Hazards Are Unpredictable
entry.content:
Extended Runs:
The Uber Labyrinth is a lengthy run, and sometimes you’ll want to take breaks, especially during high-stress sections. If you feel the need to stop, reset, or take a breather, a Portal Scroll will let you safely exit the Labyrinth. Moreover, it gives you a chance to POE currency adjust your gear, change skills, or manage your flasks and other resources.

Traps and Hazards Are Unpredictable:
While knowing the layout of the Uber Labyrinth is useful, traps and enemies can still surprise you, especially in certain layouts with unexpected traps or particularly tough monster configurations. If you’re caught off guard, Portal Scrolls give you a chance to retreat, reassess, and come back prepared.

Multiple Stages:
The Uber Labyrinth has several stages, each with its own challenges, and there are no checkpoints between these stages. If you die in one of these stages or run out of POE currency buy resources, you can use a Portal Scroll to go back to town, refuel, and come back fresh. Without this option, you risk losing all the progress you’ve made.

How Many Portal Scrolls Should You Bring?
There is no one-size-fits-all answer to how many Portal Scrolls you should bring, but here are some factors to consider when planning:

entry.title: Mmoexp CFB 25 Coins: Thriving Beyond Football
entry.content:
Embrace the Unknown: Thriving Beyond Football
Transitioning from a football career to life beyond the gridiron can be an overwhelming and uncertain experience. The familiar routines, the competitive spirit, and the sense of purpose can all seem like they are fading away. However, embracing the unknown during this transition is not only necessary but can also lead to new opportunities and growth that you may never have anticipated.

3. Seek New Experiences and Embrace Curiosity
You’ve spent years focusing on football, but the world outside offers countless opportunities to learn, grow, and explore new passions. Embrace curiosity and dive into areas of interest that were once outside your radar.

Explore New Hobbies: Whether it’s starting a business, exploring art, or diving into a new field of study, let your curiosity guide you to new and exciting places. New experiences help you find joy in the unknown and allow you to build a richer, more diverse life.
Network and Learn from Others: Seek out people who have navigated similar transitions or who are experts in fields that intrigue you. Their stories and experiences can provide valuable insights and help you expand your horizons.
4. Be Adaptable and Flexible
Life after football may not always go as CFB 25 Coins planned, and the path may be filled with unexpected turns. Flexibility is key to thriving in the unknown.

Adapt Quickly: Be ready to adjust your plans as new opportunities or challenges arise. Embrace change, even if it doesn’t align with your original goals or timeline.
Reinvent Yourself: Don’t be afraid to College Football 25 Coins redefine your personal and professional identity. Your career in football doesn’t define who you are—it’s just one chapter in your life story.

entry.title: Mmoexp Elden Ring Runes: Keep an Eye Out for NPCs and Quests
entry.content:
6. Keep an Eye Out for NPCs and Quests
Throughout your travels, you’ll encounter NPCs that offer quests or Elden Ring Runes assistance. Remaining flexible and engaging with these NPCs can yield valuable rewards and storyline progression.

Questlines: Many NPCs have intricate questlines that take you to unique areas or reward you with special items and spells. Make sure to explore thoroughly and revisit key locations to trigger quest stages.
Helpful NPCs: Some NPCs provide helpful services like healing, buffing, or selling rare items. Be sure to engage with them regularly.
7. Experiment with Fast Travel and Sites of Grace
Fast travel is essential for efficient exploration in Elden Ring. Use Sites of Grace as checkpoints and travel points.

Optimal Sites of Grace: Set up camp at key Sites of Grace to Elden Ring Runes buy ensure you have a nearby travel point when revisiting areas. This saves time when re-exploring zones or farming for specific items.
Travel Between Regions: Once you unlock the ability to fast travel, you can easily jump between regions to access new content, bosses, or special farming locations.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Feliz día!

Soy de Venezuela y me interesa conocer información de Manufactura.  Muchas gracias por recibirme en el grupo.

entry.title: The transaction types could not be updated in table TABWD
entry.content:
Dear SAP FI-AA experts,

During the implementation of SAP S/4HANA using the Greenfield approach, I am encountering an error in Asset Accounting: "The transaction types could not be updated in table" (message number ACC_AA161).

I suspect that for each "Asset Transaction ID," it is either mandatory to assign a corresponding "Transaction Type" (see the illustrations below).

Could someone kindly assist me in finding a solution to this issue?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Kind regards

Emal

entry.title: Re: Dynamically set default DATE value for input paramete...
entry.content:
Input parameter in View --> as of now, not. Only help / F4-like. 

Standard Variable in a Model --> you can use a standard one with derived value (derive from view with the date you generate based on current date).

SAC --> via a script populate a variable used in the selection of the measure in the story. 

 

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no compatible cell
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was having issues deploying my CAP application (complex) to BTP, running into a deploy issue:

Error staging application "xxx": NoCompatibleCell - Found no compatible cell

So I started from scratch with a simple application: 

- Install a fresh Trial Account

- Create a HANA DB (mapping to CF dev space)

- Create a simple CAP application in BAS, single table, simple service to display table entries

- Add hana (for production), Add mta

- Create MTA file

- Deploy to CF

All vanilla steps, explained in many blogs and CAPIRE. Nothing special, no manipulation of any package.json or any other files. 

However, no matter what I do. I always run into a CF deploy issue in the staging step stating (dmol extract) :

 
Failed to stage build: found no compatible cell with placement tag "trial" (STDERR, API)

I can't find any valuable information on this error, other than some non SAP related blogs mentioning that the stacks are not available on CF. As I did a clean install on a Trial account I assume the BTP environment is installed correctly. 

Would be great if somebody could provide some hints as I'm not able to deploy any application to BTP.

Regards,

Tom

entry.title: Re: Bom Explosion report Issue
entry.content:
Hi Ryan Crosby....

Yes STLAL  is one of the input parameter. If input  STLAL  parameter as '1' while entering  FG code , then the sub level components will be picked  with valie 1 by this function module  using STLAL  = 1 ?     suppose the sub level components  say '25001' has two values of stlal 1 and 2 .  Is there any provision to pick the stlal value 2 .

Actulay  i am working on an object in which FG is entered ( say having stlal value 1 and some sublevel components having stlal value 1  and other having stlal value 2 the latest )   i want to pick the report based on latest stlal value..

Regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
First of all, thank you for the "ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022"!

I was able to successfully install and login to S/4 system via SAP GUI as well as Eclipse with ADT and BWMT.

Is there any way to login to the backend database of this S/4 (DB Host: vhcala4hci) via Eclipse HANA modelling tools? I want to try out a hybrid scenario where one can consume a HANA view into BW modelling for reporting.

Regards,

Mandar Damle

entry.title: Re: Goods movement bin not determined or entered-Message...
entry.content:
I also had the same issue.
I did all the configuration related to Warehouse Process Type.


1. Define Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

2. Assign Storage Types to Storage Type Search Sequence

3. Define Putaway Control Indicator

4. Specify Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

5. Define Warehouse Process Type

6. Define Control Indicators for Determining Warehouse Process Types

7. Determine Warehouse Process Type

Even after that had the same problem.

Then i realized that i had not create a Storage bin for 9010 storage type to receive the goods while Unloading.

then i manually created storage bin GR-ZONE using the tcode /n/SCWM/LS01.

Then the Unloading issue solved.

 

Regards

Naeeum

entry.title: Re: EWM SMQ2--Error during mapping: material XXXXXXX does...
entry.content:
I also had the issue while creating Inbound delivery.
When i checked the log in SLG1 then the error was ' Could not determine the EWM data for: Availability Group XXXX 001 FF N'


Then maintained the customizations .
Path: SCM Extended Warehouse Management--Extended Warehouse Management--Goods Receipt Process--Configure Availability Group for Putaway

Completed all the steps.

 

Regards
Naeeum

entry.title: Re: Basic Start Date of Preventive Maintenance Order keep...
entry.content:
Hi Dan, Thank you for your time. Please see the details of the Maintenance Plan and the Maintenance Strategy in the plan.

I also thought it may be related to the measurement documents for the equipment but when I checked some of the WOs that were not rescheduled, the equipments did not have measurement documents as well, similar to the equipment associated with the WO I posted originally.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Migrating from CR9 nowadays
entry.content:
Hello,

The problem is the platform support for your app in VS 2022 and CR 9's database support.

See this for more info:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

And the Platform support info:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

CR for VS latest SP 36 supports Windows Framework 4.7 and 4.8. That VC 2005 runtime I believe is 1.x Framework.

The problem is CR has always been linked to Microsoft, so if MS end of life any of their products CR follows suit.

So to be Windows Compliant you must keep your app up to date.

I wrote a blog on upgrading to current versions:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

Sorry it's C# but the same upgrade info relates to VB.

You don't have to embed your reports into the app. First link has a few sample app's to show you how to load the unmanaged reports from a folder.

As for the main potential issue you may run into is the DB source you are using, ODBC, OLE DB, Native client etc.

CR embeds the DB client used to connect into the RPT file, so when you load the report it knows what Client to look for.

Depending on your data source depends on if you could have issues or not, CR dynamically loads the DB info on first load, open a report in CR 2020, 64 bit version and then Database, Set Location and connect to your data source.

Click on Verify Data Database, if any messages or database mapping UI pop's up then you have no option but to update each report manually. Unfortunately the DSK is not capable of updating the Data source, it can do it at run time but the updated info will not be saved in the Report.

So I can't say what possible issues you may have without more details.

All I can suggest is upgrade your app to VS 2022 using the 64 bit installer:

CR for Visual Studio SP36 64b installer (VS 2022 and above)

And set your Framework to 4.7 or 4.8, only one supported now.

Run your app and see what happens.... Note also you should set the Platform to x64, don't use AnyCPU, it can cause install problems on users PC's. CR uses the App Platform and not the PC's platform, although not much of an issue anymore since most users us 64 bit Windows, 32 bit OS's are no longer supported.

If you do need to update your reports get CR 2020 and use it to do the updating, if there are that many reports hire a student to do it for you....

You can get CR 2020 here:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/crystal-reports.html#try-now

Note also that because CR 2020 is 64 bit your data source will need to support 64 bit Client.

Hope that helps?

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 : Auto resize ?
entry.content:
What you are experiencing is normal behaviour.

By tabbing to the height CR assumes you want to retain the same ratio.

If you don't want to change it then click OK without tabbing.

This is by design and normal behaviour.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAP...
entry.content:
Consider CDS views. Extractors are obsolete 🙂

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/5418de55938d1d22e10000000a44147b.html --> S/4 CDS views for extraction

Check OSS 2500202 as well. 

I also found a nice blog while searching: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/cds-views-vs-lis-in-pm-how-to-use-cds-views-to-create-your-own-queries/ba-p/13445774

Martin

entry.title: Re: Bom Explosion report Issue
entry.content: @RKVASH any references to sub BOMs within a FG BOM is part of the master data, so the system will explode whichever alternative BOM is assigned as a subcomponent.

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 : The document could not be opened
entry.content:
Hello,

The likely cause is something has changed/been updated in the RPT file itself that CR 2020 doesn't know how to resolve.

This can be caused by the differences between versions, R&D does update the internal structure of the RPT file from time to time, expanding area's with enhanced details etc.

When opening a report there is an import routine that happens each time, the EXE reads the history and depending on what info it gets depend on what it does.

For example here's the SDK reading the history of the report:

What you are doing is updating that history when saving in CR 2020, then opening in CR 2013 and resaving it.

It is "cleaning" up the RPT file so it's import routine knows what to do with it now in CR 2020.

FYI - here's a KBA on this error:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2914156

If you want send me a link to download one of your reports, I'll have a look at it and see if I can duplicate the issue, if I can I'll pass it onto SAP R&D to have a look at it. And open it in my main test app and do some checks to see if it catches anything.

Do your reports have Saved data when you save them? It could be Database Client related possibly.

When you do open the reports in CR 2020 Click on Database menu, Verify Database and then save the reports, that may fix the issue. Verify Database does more than just the database info, it does check a few other things as well.

What is your data source cleint, ODBC, OLE DB, JDBC, Etc.?

Unfortunately there is no sure way to "fix" this, especially when it seems to be random.

entry.title: B1IF: Call POST http in json format with error
entry.content:
Hi There,

I would like to perform an http call in json format to call an API that is exposed.
And it work fine in Postman.
See screenshot:

And I refer to this post create a B1if scenario
[B1if: How to call PUT Http in json format](https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/b1if-how-to-call-put-http-in-json-format/qaq-p/11585506)
The step is, see attachment (step.xml).

And I got the below error:

<Payload Role="C" id="atom1" statusNo="HTTP400" statusMsg="Bad Request" reference="atom3" sysid="" payload="atom3" throwException="false">
<http.header/>
<httaError xmlns="urn:com.sap.b1i.xcellerator:upltdoc" HTTPcode="400" HTTPmsg="Bad Request">
<exceptionMsg>com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE212 Error while performing HTTP call; HTTP status is 400, Bad Request</exceptionMsg>
<returnData><!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN""http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/strict.dtd"> <HTML><HEAD><TITLE>Bad Request</TITLE> <META HTTP-EQUIV="Content-Type" Content="text/html; charset=us-ascii"></HEAD> <BODY><h2>Bad Request - Invalid Header</h2> <hr><p>HTTP Error 400. The request has an invalid header name.</p> </BODY></HTML> </returnData>
</httaError>
</Payload>

 

Any suggestion?

BR

Robin

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
In BW you install content, you enhance it (like 0BPARTNER) and then if you re-install, while for example there is a change in the content you would like to implement, you changes will be overwritten. You also get a warning when installing content in BW, rt? Have the option to choose. Once content was installed, it was never re-installed.

Contrary to DSP.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAP...
entry.content:
Thank you, Martin.

Currently, we are still on ECC and might take a few more years to transition to S/4HANA, where we can leverage CDS views instead of extractors. In the meantime, we are seeking a solution to continue utilizing SAPI extractors or any insights on SAP's plans for supporting delta functionality for extractors moving forward in replication flow.
